verses.latin_vulgate: Eliseus autem locutus est ad mulierem cujus vivere fecerat filium, dicens : Surge, vade tu et domus tua, et peregrinare ubicumque repereris : vocavit enim Dominus famem, et veniet super terram septem annis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וֶאֱלִישָׁ֡ע דִּבֶּ֣ר אֶל־הָאִשָּׁה֩ אֲשֶׁר־הֶחֱיָ֨ה אֶת־בְּנָ֜הּ לֵאמֹ֗ר ק֤וּמִי וּלְכִי֙ (אתי) [אַ֣תְּ] וּבֵיתֵ֔ךְ וְג֖וּרִי בַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר תָּג֑וּרִי כִּֽי־קָרָ֤א יְהֹוָה֙ לָרָעָ֔ב וְגַם־בָּ֥א אֶל־הָאָ֖רֶץ שֶׁ֥בַע שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Eliseus spoke to the woman, whose son he had restored to life, saying: Arise, and go thou, and thy household, and sojourn wheresoever thou canst find: for the Lord hath called a famine, and it shall come upon the land seven years.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elisa puhui vaimolle, jonka pojan hän oli herättänyt, ja sanoi: nouse ja matkusta pois huoneines, ja ole outona, kussas saat; sillä Herra on kutsuva nälän, joka on tuleva maalle seitsemäksi vuodeksi.
verses.king_james: Then spake Elisha unto the woman, whose son he had restored to life, saying, Arise, and go thou and thine household, and sojourn wheresoever thou canst sojourn: for the Lord hath called for a famine; and it shall also come upon the land seven years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quæ surrexit, et fecit juxta verbum hominis Dei : et vadens cum domo sua, peregrinata est in terra Philisthiim diebus multis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתָּ֙קׇם֙ הָאִשָּׁ֔ה וַתַּ֕עַשׂ כִּדְבַ֖ר אִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים וַתֵּ֤לֶךְ הִיא֙ וּבֵיתָ֔הּ וַתָּ֥גׇר בְּאֶרֶץ־פְּלִשְׁתִּ֖ים שֶׁ֥בַע שָׁנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And she arose, and did according to the word of the man of God: and going with her household, she sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaimo nousi ja teki niinkuin Jumalan mies sanoi, ja matkusti huoneinensa, ja oli outona Philistealaisten maalla seitsemän vuotta.
verses.king_james: And the woman arose, and did after the saying of the man of God: and she went with her household, and sojourned in the land of the Philistines seven years.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque finiti essent anni septem, reversa est mulier de terra Philisthiim : et egressa est ut interpellaret regem pro domo sua, et pro agris suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֗י מִקְצֵה֙ שֶׁ֣בַע שָׁנִ֔ים וַתָּ֥שׇׁב הָאִשָּׁ֖ה מֵאֶ֣רֶץ פְּלִשְׁתִּ֑ים וַתֵּצֵא֙ לִצְעֹ֣ק אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ אֶל־בֵּיתָ֖הּ וְאֶל־שָׂדָֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when the seven years were ended, the woman returned out of the land of the Philistines, and she went forth to speak to the king for her house and for her lands.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja seitsemän vuoden perästä tuli vaimo Philistealaisten maalta jälleen. Ja hän meni rukoilemaan kuninkaalta huonettansa ja peltoansa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass at the seven years’ end, that the woman returned out of the land of the Philistines: and she went forth to cry unto the king for her house and for her land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rex autem loquebatur cum Giezi puero viri Dei, dicens : Narra mihi omnia magnalia quæ fecit Eliseus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַמֶּ֗לֶךְ מְדַבֵּר֙ אֶל־גֵּ֣חֲזִ֔י נַ֥עַר אִישׁ־הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים לֵאמֹ֑ר סַפְּרָה־נָּ֣א לִ֔י אֵ֥ת כׇּל־הַגְּדֹל֖וֹת אֲשֶׁר־עָשָׂ֥ה אֱלִישָֽׁע׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the king talked with Giezi, the servant of the man of God, saying: Tell me all the great things that Eliseus hath done.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin puhui kuningas Gehatsin, Jumalan miehen palvelian kanssa ja sanoi: juttele minulle kaikki ne suuret teot, mitkä Elisa on tehnyt!
verses.king_james: And the king talked with Gehazi the servant of the man of God, saying, Tell me, I pray thee, all the great things that Elisha hath done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque ille narraret regi quomodo mortuum suscitasset, apparuit mulier cujus vivificaverat filium, clamans ad regem pro domo sua, et pro agris suis. Dixitque Giezi : Domine mi rex, hæc est mulier, et hic est filius ejus quem suscitavit Eliseus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֠יְהִ֠י ה֥וּא מְסַפֵּ֣ר לַמֶּ֘לֶךְ֮ אֵ֣ת אֲשֶׁר־הֶחֱיָ֣ה אֶת־הַמֵּת֒ וְהִנֵּ֨ה הָאִשָּׁ֜ה אֲשֶׁר־הֶחֱיָ֤ה אֶת־בְּנָהּ֙ צֹעֶ֣קֶת אֶל־הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ עַל־בֵּיתָ֖הּ וְעַל־שָׂדָ֑הּ וַיֹּ֤אמֶר גֵּֽחֲזִי֙ אֲדֹנִ֣י הַמֶּ֔לֶךְ זֹ֚את הָֽאִשָּׁ֔ה וְזֶה־בְּנָ֖הּ אֲשֶׁר־הֶחֱיָ֥ה אֱלִישָֽׁע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he was telling the king how he had raised one dead to life, the woman appeared, whose son he had restored to life, crying to the king for her house, and her lands. And Giezi said: My lord, O king, this is the woman, and this is her son, whom Eliseus raised to life.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuin hän ilmoitti kuninkaalle, kuinka hän oli kuolleen virvoittanut, ja katso, vaimo, jonka pojan hän oli kuolleista herättänyt, huusi kuninkaan tykö huoneensa ja peltonsa tähden. Niin sanoi Gehatsi: herrani kuningas, tämä on vaimo, ja tämä on hänen poikansa, jonka Elisa kuolleista herätti.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as he was telling the king how he had restored a dead body to life, that, behold, the woman, whose son he had restored to life, cried to the king for her house and for her land. And Gehazi said, My lord, O king, this is the woman, and this is her son, whom Elisha restored to life.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogavit rex mulierem : quæ narravit ei. Deditque ei rex eunuchum unum, dicens : Restitue ei omnia quæ sua sunt, et universos reditus agrorum, a die qua reliquit terram usque ad præsens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁאַ֥ל הַמֶּ֛לֶךְ לָאִשָּׁ֖ה וַתְּסַפֶּר־ל֑וֹ וַיִּתֶּן־לָ֣הּ הַמֶּ֩לֶךְ֩ סָרִ֨יס אֶחָ֜ד לֵאמֹ֗ר הָשֵׁ֤יב אֶת־כׇּל־אֲשֶׁר־לָהּ֙ וְאֵת֙ כׇּל־תְּבוּאֹ֣ת הַשָּׂדֶ֔ה מִיּ֛וֹם עׇזְבָ֥הֿ אֶת־הָאָ֖רֶץ וְעַד־עָֽתָּה׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And the king asked the woman: and she told him. And the king appointed her an eunuch, saying: Restore her all that is hers, and all the revenues of the lands, from the day that she left the land to this present.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas kysyi vaimolta, ja hän jutteli hänelle sen. Niin kuningas antoi hänelle yhden palvelioistansa ja sanoi: anna hänen saada kaikki, mitä hänen omansa on, ja kaikki tulot pelloista, siitä päivästä, jona hän maan jätti, niin tähän asti.
verses.king_james: And when the king asked the woman, she told him. So the king appointed unto her a certain officer, saying, Restore all that was hers, and all the fruits of the field since the day that she left the land, even until now.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit quoque Eliseus Damascum, et Benadad rex Syriæ ægrotabat : nuntiaveruntque ei, dicentes : Venit vir Dei huc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹ֤א אֱלִישָׁע֙ דַּמֶּ֔שֶׂק וּבֶן־הֲדַ֥ד מֶלֶךְ־אֲרָ֖ם חֹלֶ֑ה וַיֻּגַּד־ל֣וֹ לֵאמֹ֔ר בָּ֛א אִ֥ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֖ים עַד־הֵֽנָּה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Eliseus also came to Damascus, and Benadad, king of Syria was sick; and they told him, saying: The man of God is come hither.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Elisa tuli Damaskuun, ja Benhadad Syrian kuningas sairasti, ja hänelle oli ilmoitettu ja sanottu, että Jumalan mies on tullut tänne.
verses.king_james: And Elisha came to Damascus; and Ben–hadad the king of Syria was sick; and it was told him, saying, The man of God is come hither.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait rex ad Hazaël : Tolle tecum munera, et vade in occursum viri Dei, et consule Dominum per eum, dicens : Si evadere potero de infirmitate mea hac ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֨אמֶר הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ אֶל־חֲזָהאֵ֗ל קַ֤ח בְּיָדְךָ֙ מִנְחָ֔ה וְלֵ֕ךְ לִקְרַ֖את אִ֣ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִ֑ים וְדָרַשְׁתָּ֨ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֤ה מֵאוֹתוֹ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הַאֶחְיֶ֖ה מֵחֳלִ֥י זֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to Hazael: Take with thee presents, and go to meet the man of God, and consult the Lord by him, saying: Can I recover of this my illness?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kuningas sanoi Hasaelille: ota lahjoja myötäs ja mene Jumalan miestä vastaan, ja kysy hänen kauttansa neuvoa Herralta ja sano: parantunenko minä tästä taudista?
verses.king_james: And the king said unto Hazael, Take a present in thine hand, and go, meet the man of God, and enquire of the Lord by him, saying, Shall I recover of this disease?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ivit igitur Hazaël in occursum ejus, habens secum munera, et omnia bona Damasci, onera quadraginta camelorum. Cumque stetisset coram eo, ait : Filius tuus Benadad rex Syriæ misit me ad te, dicens : Si sanari potero de infirmitate mea hac ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֣לֶךְ חֲזָאֵל֮ לִקְרָאתוֹ֒ וַיִּקַּ֨ח מִנְחָ֤ה בְיָדוֹ֙ וְכׇל־ט֣וּב דַּמֶּ֔שֶׂק מַשָּׂ֖א אַרְבָּעִ֣ים גָּמָ֑ל וַיָּבֹא֙ וַיַּעֲמֹ֣ד לְפָנָ֔יו וַיֹּ֗אמֶר בִּנְךָ֨ בֶן־הֲדַ֤ד מֶֽלֶךְ־אֲרָם֙ שְׁלָחַ֤נִי אֵלֶ֙יךָ֙ לֵאמֹ֔ר הַאֶחְיֶ֖ה מֵחֳלִ֥י זֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Hazael went to meet him, taking with him presents, and all the good things of Damascus, the burdens of forty camels. And when he stood before him, he said: Thy son, Benadad, the king of Syria, hath sent me to thee, saying: Can I recover of this my illness?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Hasael meni häntä vastaan ja otti lahjoja myötänsä ja kaikkinaista Damaskun hyvyyttä neljäkymmentä kamelin kuormaa; ja kuin hän tuli, seisoi hän hänen edessänsä ja sanoi: Benhadad, sinun poikas, Syrian kuningas, lähetti minun sinun tykös ja käski sinulle sanoa: parantunenko minä tästä taudista?
verses.king_james: So Hazael went to meet him, and took a present with him, even of every good thing of Damascus, forty camels’ burden, and came and stood before him, and said, Thy son Ben–hadad king of Syria hath sent me to thee, saying, Shall I recover of this disease?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque ei Eliseus : Vade, dic ei : Sanaberis : porro ostendit mihi Dominus quia morte morietur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר אֵלָיו֙ אֱלִישָׁ֔ע לֵ֥ךְ אֱמׇר־[ל֖וֹ] (לא) חָיֹ֣ה תִֽחְיֶ֑ה וְהִרְאַ֥נִי יְהֹוָ֖ה כִּי־מ֥וֹת יָמֽוּת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Eliseus said to him: Go tell him: Thou shalt recover: but the Lord hath shewed me that he shall surely die.
verses.finnish_biblia: Elisa sanoi hänelle: mene ja sano hänelle: kyllä sinä paranisit; mutta kuitenkin on Herra ilmoittanut minulle, että hänen pitää totisesti kuoleman.
verses.king_james: And Elisha said unto him, Go, say unto him, Thou mayest certainly recover: howbeit the Lord hath shewed me that he shall surely die.

verses.latin_vulgate: Stetique cum eo, et conturbatus est usque ad suffusionem vultus : flevitque vir Dei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲמֵ֥ד אֶת־פָּנָ֖יו וַיָּ֣שֶׂם עַד־בֹּ֑שׁ וַיֵּ֖בְךְּ אִ֥ישׁ הָאֱלֹהִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he stood with him, and was troubled so far as to blush: and the man of God wept.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja (Jumalan mies) katsoi terävästi hänen päällensä, siihenasti että hän häpesi, sitte itki Jumalan mies.
verses.king_james: And he settled his countenance stedfastly, until he was ashamed: and the man of God wept.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui Hazaël ait : Quare dominus meus flet ? At ille dixit : Quia scio quæ facturus sis filiis Israël mala. Civitates eorum munitas igne succendes, et juvenes eorum interficies gladio, et parvulos eorum elides, et prægnantes divides.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר חֲזָאֵ֔ל מַדּ֖וּעַ אֲדֹנִ֣י בֹכֶ֑ה וַיֹּ֡אמֶר כִּֽי־יָדַ֡עְתִּי אֵ֣ת אֲשֶׁר־תַּעֲשֶׂה֩ לִבְנֵ֨י יִשְׂרָאֵ֜ל רָעָ֗ה מִבְצְרֵיהֶ֞ם תְּשַׁלַּ֤ח בָּאֵשׁ֙ וּבַחֻֽרֵיהֶם֙ בַּחֶ֣רֶב תַּהֲרֹ֔ג וְעֹלְלֵיהֶ֣ם תְּרַטֵּ֔שׁ וְהָרֹתֵיהֶ֖ם תְּבַקֵּֽעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Hazael said to him: Why doth my lord weep? And he said: Because I know the evil that thou wilt do to the children of Israel. Their strong cities thou wilt burn with fire, and their young men thou wilt kill with the sword, and thou wilt dash their children, and rip up their pregnant women.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin Hasael sanoi: miksi herrani itkee? Ja hän sanoi: minä tiedän, mitä pahuutta sinun pitää tekemän Israelin lapsille: sinun pitää polttaman heidän väkevät kaupunkinsa tulella, ja heidän nuoret miehensä tappaman miekalla, ja surmaaman heidän lapsensa, ja heidän raskaat vaimonsa halkaiseman.
verses.king_james: And Hazael said, Why weepeth my lord? And he answered, Because I know the evil that thou wilt do unto the children of Israel: their strong holds wilt thou set on fire, and their young men wilt thou slay with the sword, and wilt dash their children, and rip up their women with child.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Hazaël : Quid enim sum servus tuus canis, ut faciam rem istam magnam ? Et ait Eliseus : Ostendit mihi Dominus te regem Syriæ fore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר חֲזָהאֵ֔ל כִּ֣י מָ֤ה עַבְדְּךָ֙ הַכֶּ֔לֶב כִּ֣י יַעֲשֶׂ֔ה הַדָּבָ֥ר הַגָּד֖וֹל הַזֶּ֑ה וַיֹּ֣אמֶר אֱלִישָׁ֔ע הִרְאַ֧נִי יְהֹוָ֛ה אֹתְךָ֖ מֶ֥לֶךְ עַל־אֲרָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Hazael said: But what am I, thy servant, a dog, that I should do this great thing? And Eliseus said: The Lord hath shewed me that thou shalt be king of Syria.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hasael sanoi: mikä on sinun palvelias tämä koira, että hän niin suuria asioita tekisi? Elisa sanoi: Herra on minulle ilmoittanut sinun tulevan Syrian kuninkaaksi.
verses.king_james: And Hazael said, But what, is thy servant a dog, that he should do this great thing? And Elisha answered, The Lord hath shewed me that thou shalt be king over Syria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum recessisset ab Eliseo, venit ad dominum suum. Qui ait ei : Quid dixit tibi Eliseus ? At ille respondit : Dixit mihi : Recipies sanitatem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֣לֶךְ ׀ מֵאֵ֣ת אֱלִישָׁ֗ע וַיָּבֹא֙ אֶל־אֲדֹנָ֔יו וַיֹּ֣אמֶר ל֔וֹ מָה־אָמַ֥ר לְךָ֖ אֱלִישָׁ֑ע וַיֹּ֕אמֶר אָ֥מַר לִ֖י חָיֹ֥ה תִֽחְיֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when he was departed from Eliseus he came to his master, who said to him: What said Eliseus to thee? And he answered: He told me: Thou shalt recover.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän meni pois Elisan tyköä ja tuli herransa tykö, ja hän sanoi hänelle: mitä Elisa sanoi sinulle? Hän vastasi: hän sanoi minulle sinun paranevan.
verses.king_james: So he departed from Elisha, and came to his master; who said to him, What said Elisha to thee? And he answered, He told me that thou shouldest surely recover.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cumque venisset dies altera, tulit stragulum, et infudit aquam, et expandit super faciem ejus : quo mortuo, regnavit Hazaël pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י מִֽמׇּחֳרָ֗ת וַיִּקַּ֤ח הַמַּכְבֵּר֙ וַיִּטְבֹּ֣ל בַּמַּ֔יִם וַיִּפְרֹ֥שׂ עַל־פָּנָ֖יו וַיָּמֹ֑ת וַיִּמְלֹ֥ךְ חֲזָהאֵ֖ל תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And on the next day, he took a blanket, and poured water on it, and spread it upon his face: and he died, and Hazael reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta toisena päivänä otti hän yhden makausvaatteen ja kasti veteen, ja levitti hänen kasvoinsa päälle, ja hän kuoli; ja Hasael tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: And it came to pass on the morrow, that he took a thick cloth, and dipped it in water, and spread it on his face, so that he died: and Hazael reigned in his stead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anno quinto Joram filii Achab regis Israël, et Josaphat regis Juda, regnavit Joram filius Josaphat rex Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבִשְׁנַ֣ת חָמֵ֗שׁ לְיוֹרָ֤ם בֶּן־אַחְאָב֙ מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל וִיהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט מֶ֣לֶךְ יְהוּדָ֑ה מָלַ֛ךְ יְהוֹרָ֥ם בֶּן־יְהוֹשָׁפָ֖ט מֶ֥לֶךְ יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the fifth year of Joram, son of Achab, king of Israel, and of Josaphat, king of Juda, reigned Joram, son of Josaphat, king of Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joramin Ahabin pojan Israelin kuninkaan viidentenä vuonna, ja Josaphatin Juudan kuninkaan (kuolemavuonna) tuli Joram Josaphatin poika Juudan kuninkaaksi.
verses.king_james: And in the fifth year of Joram the son of Ahab king of Israel, Jehoshaphat being then king of Judah, Jehoram the son of Jehoshaphat king of Judah began to reign.

verses.latin_vulgate: Triginta duorum annorum erat cum regnare cœpisset, et octo annis regnavit in Jerusalem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֶּן־שְׁלֹשִׁ֥ים וּשְׁתַּ֛יִם שָׁנָ֖ה הָיָ֣ה בְמׇלְכ֑וֹ וּשְׁמֹנֶ֣ה (שנה) [שָׁנִ֔ים] מָלַ֖ךְ בִּירוּשָׁלָֽ͏ִם׃
verses.douay_rheims: He was two and thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän oli kahden ajastajan vanha neljättäkymmentä tullessansa kuninkaaksi, ja hallitsi kahdeksan ajastaikaa Jerusalemissa,
verses.king_james: Thirty and two years old was he when he began to reign; and he reigned eight years in Jerusalem.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ambulavitque in viis regum Israël, sicut ambulaverat domus Achab : filia enim Achab erat uxor ejus : et fecit quod malum est in conspectu Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֜לֶךְ בְּדֶ֣רֶךְ ׀ מַלְכֵ֣י יִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל כַּאֲשֶׁ֤ר עָשׂוּ֙ בֵּ֣ית אַחְאָ֔ב כִּ֚י בַּת־אַחְאָ֔ב הָ֥יְתָה לּ֖וֹ לְאִשָּׁ֑ה וַיַּ֥עַשׂ הָרַ֖ע בְּעֵינֵ֥י יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he walked in the ways of the kings of Israel, as the house of Achab had walked: for the daughter of Achab was his wife: and he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja vaelsi Israelin kuningasten teillä, niin kuin Ahabin huone teki: sillä Ahabin tytär oli hänen emäntänsä, ja hän teki pahaa Herran edessä.
verses.king_james: And he walked in the way of the kings of Israel, as did the house of Ahab: for the daughter of Ahab was his wife: and he did evil in the sight of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noluit autem Dominus disperdere Judam, propter David servum suum, sicut promiserat ei, ut daret illi lucernam, et filiis ejus cunctis diebus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹא־אָבָ֤ה יְהֹוָה֙ לְהַשְׁחִ֣ית אֶת־יְהוּדָ֔ה לְמַ֖עַן דָּוִ֣ד עַבְדּ֑וֹ כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר אָמַר־ל֗וֹ לָתֵ֨ת ל֥וֹ נִ֛יר לְבָנָ֖יו כׇּל־הַיָּמִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the Lord would not destroy Juda, for David his servant's sake, as he had promised him, to give him a light, and to his children always.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta palveliansa Davidin tähden ei Herra tahtonut hävittää Juudaa, niinkuin hän oli hänelle sanonut, että hän oli antava hänelle yhden valkeuden ja hänen lapsillensa joka aika.
verses.king_james: Yet the Lord would not destroy Judah for David his servant’s sake, as he promised him to give him alway a light, and to his children.

verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus ejus recessit Edom ne esset sub Juda, et constituit sibi regem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּיָמָיו֙ פָּשַׁ֣ע אֱד֔וֹם מִתַּ֖חַת יַד־יְהוּדָ֑ה וַיַּמְלִ֥כוּ עֲלֵיהֶ֖ם מֶֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: In his days Edom revolted from being under Juda, and made themselves a king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hänen aikanansa luopuivat Edomilaiset Juudasta ja tekivät itsellensä kuninkaan.
verses.king_james: In his days Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah, and made a king over themselves.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venitque Joram Seira, et omnes currus cum eo : et surrexit nocte, percussitque Idumæos qui eum circumdederant, et principes curruum : populus autem fugit in tabernacula sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּעֲבֹ֤ר יוֹרָם֙ צָעִ֔ירָה וְכׇל־הָרֶ֖כֶב עִמּ֑וֹ וַֽיְהִי־ה֞וּא קָ֣ם לַ֗יְלָה וַיַּכֶּ֨ה אֶת־אֱד֜וֹם הַסֹּבֵ֤יב אֵלָיו֙ וְאֵת֙ שָׂרֵ֣י הָרֶ֔כֶב וַיָּ֥נׇס הָעָ֖ם לְאֹהָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Joram came to Seira, and all the chariots with him: and he arose in the night, and defeated the Edomites that had surrounded him, and the captains of the chariots, but the people fled into their tents.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä Joram oli matkustanut Zairiin ja kaikki vaunut hänen kanssansa, ja nousi yöllä ja löi Edomilaiset, jotka hänen ympärillänsä olivat, niin myös vaunuin päämiehet, niin että kansa pakeni majoihinsa.
verses.king_james: So Joram went over to Zair, and all the chariots with him: and he rose by night, and smote the Edomites which compassed him about, and the captains of the chariots: and the people fled into their tents.

verses.latin_vulgate: Recessit ergo Edom ne esset sub Juda, usque ad diem hanc. Tunc recessit et Lobna in tempore illo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּפְשַׁ֣ע אֱד֗וֹם מִתַּ֙חַת֙ יַד־יְהוּדָ֔ה עַ֖ד הַיּ֣וֹם הַזֶּ֑ה אָ֛ז תִּפְשַׁ֥ע לִבְנָ֖ה בָּעֵ֥ת הַהִֽיא׃
verses.douay_rheims: So Edom revolted from being under Juda, unto this day. Then Lobna also revolted at the same time.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sentähden luopuivat Edomilaiset Juudasta tähän päivään asti; silloin myös Libna luopui siihen aikaan.
verses.king_james: Yet Edom revolted from under the hand of Judah unto this day. Then Libnah revolted at the same time.

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem sermonum Joram, et universa quæ fecit, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro verborum dierum regum Juda ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֛תֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י יוֹרָ֖ם וְכׇל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֑ה הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: But the rest of the acts of Joram, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä enempi Joramista sanomista on ja kaikista niistä, mitä hän tehnyt on: eikö se ole kirjoitettu Juudan kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: And the rest of the acts of Joram, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dormivit Joram cum patribus suis, sepultusque est cum eis in civitate David, et regnavit Ochozias filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֤ב יוֹרָם֙ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֔יו וַיִּקָּבֵ֥ר עִם־אֲבֹתָ֖יו בְּעִ֣יר דָּוִ֑ד וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ אֲחַזְיָ֥הוּ בְנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Joram slept with his fathers, and was buried with them in the city of David, and Ochozias, his son, reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Joram nukkui isäinsä kanssa ja haudattiin isäinsä kanssa Davidin kaupunkiin. Ja Ahasia hänen poikansa tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: And Joram slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David: and Ahaziah his son reigned in his stead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Anno duodecimo Joram filii Achab regis Israël regnavit Ochozias filius Joram regis Judæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּשְׁנַת֙ שְׁתֵּים־עֶשְׂרֵ֣ה שָׁנָ֔ה לְיוֹרָ֥ם בֶּן־אַחְאָ֖ב מֶ֣לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל מָלַ֛ךְ אֲחַזְיָ֥הוּ בֶן־יְהוֹרָ֖ם מֶ֥לֶךְ יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the twelfth year of Joram, the son of Achab, king of Israel, reigned Ochozias, son of Joram, king of Juda.
verses.finnish_biblia: Joramin Ahabin pojan Israelin kuninkaan toisena vuonna toistakymmentä tuli Ahasia Joramin poika Juudan kuninkaaksi.
verses.king_james: In the twelfth year of Joram the son of Ahab king of Israel did Ahaziah the son of Jehoram king of Judah begin to reign.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viginti duorum annorum erat Ochozias cum regnare cœpisset, et uno anno regnavit in Jerusalem : nomen matris ejus Athalia filia Amri regis Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֶּן־עֶשְׂרִ֨ים וּשְׁתַּ֤יִם שָׁנָה֙ אֲחַזְיָ֣הוּ בְמׇלְכ֔וֹ וְשָׁנָ֣ה אַחַ֔ת מָלַ֖ךְ בִּירוּשָׁלָ֑͏ִם וְשֵׁ֤ם אִמּוֹ֙ עֲתַלְיָ֔הוּ בַּת־עׇמְרִ֖י מֶ֥לֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Ochozias was two and twenty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Athalia the daughter of Amri king of Israel.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Ahasia oli kahdenkolmattakymmentä vuoden vanha, koska hän tuli kuninkaaksi, ja hallitsi yhden vuoden Jerusalemissa; hänen äitinsä nimi oli Atalia Omrin Israelin kuninkaan tytär.
verses.king_james: Two and twenty years old was Ahaziah when he began to reign; and he reigned one year in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Athaliah, the daughter of Omri king of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ambulavit in viis domus Achab : et fecit quod malum est coram Domino, sicut domus Achab : gener enim domus Achab fuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֗לֶךְ בְּדֶ֙רֶךְ֙ בֵּ֣ית אַחְאָ֔ב וַיַּ֧עַשׂ הָרַ֛ע בְּעֵינֵ֥י יְהֹוָ֖ה כְּבֵ֣ית אַחְאָ֑ב כִּ֛י חֲתַ֥ן בֵּית־אַחְאָ֖ב הֽוּא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he walked in the ways of the house of Achab: and he did evil before the Lord, as did the house of Achab: for he was the son in law of the house of Achab.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän vaelsi Ahabin huoneen teillä ja teki pahaa Herran edessä, niinkuin Ahabin huone; sillä hän oli Ahabin huoneen vävy.
verses.king_james: And he walked in the way of the house of Ahab, and did evil in the sight of the Lord, as did the house of Ahab: for he was the son in law of the house of Ahab.

verses.latin_vulgate: Abiit quoque cum Joram filio Achab ad præliandum contra Hazaël regem Syriæ in Ramoth Galaad, et vulneraverunt Syri Joram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֵּ֜לֶךְ אֶת־יוֹרָ֣ם בֶּן־אַחְאָ֗ב לַמִּלְחָמָ֛ה עִם־חֲזָאֵ֥ל מֶלֶךְ־אֲרָ֖ם בְּרָמֹ֣ת גִּלְעָ֑ד וַיַּכּ֥וּ אֲרַמִּ֖ים אֶת־יוֹרָֽם׃
verses.douay_rheims: He went also with Joram, son of Achab, to fight against Hazael, king of Syria, in Ramoth Galaad, and the Syrians wounded Joram:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän meni Joramin Ahabin pojan kanssa sotimaan Hasaelia Syrian kuningasta vastaan, Gileadin Ramotissa; mutta Syrialaiset löivät Joramin.
verses.king_james: And he went with Joram the son of Ahab to the war against Hazael king of Syria in Ramoth–gilead; and the Syrians wounded Joram.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui reversus est ut curaretur in Jezrahel, quia vulneraverant eum Syri in Ramoth præliantem contra Hazaël regem Syriæ. Porro Ochozias filius Joram rex Juda descendit invisere Joram filium Achab in Jezrahel, quia ægrotabat ibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֩שׇׁב֩ יוֹרָ֨ם הַמֶּ֜לֶךְ לְהִתְרַפֵּ֣א בְיִזְרְעֶ֗אל מִן־הַמַּכִּים֙ אֲשֶׁ֨ר יַכֻּ֤הוּ אֲרַמִּים֙ בָּרָמָ֔ה בְּהִלָּ֣חֲמ֔וֹ אֶת־חֲזָהאֵ֖ל מֶ֣לֶךְ אֲרָ֑ם וַאֲחַזְיָ֨הוּ בֶן־יְהוֹרָ֜ם מֶ֣לֶךְ יְהוּדָ֗ה יָרַ֡ד לִרְא֞וֹת אֶת־יוֹרָ֧ם בֶּן־אַחְאָ֛ב בְּיִזְרְעֶ֖אל כִּי־חֹלֶ֥ה הֽוּא׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And he went back to be healed, in Jezrahel: because the Syrians had wounded him in Ramoth, when he fought against Hazael, king of Syria And Ochozias, the son of Joram, king of Juda, went down to visit Joram, the son of Achab, in Jezrahel, because he was sick there.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin palasi kuningas Joram antamaan parantaa itsensä Jisreelissä niistä haavoista, jotka Syrialaiset löivät häneen Ramassa, hänen soteissa Hasaelin Syrian kuninkaan kanssa; ja Ahasia Joramin poika, Juudan kuningas, tuli katsomaan Joramia Ahabin poikaa Jisreeliin; sillä hän sairasti.
verses.king_james: And king Joram went back to be healed in Jezreel of the wounds which the Syrians had given him at Ramah, when he fought against Hazael king of Syria. And Ahaziah the son of Jehoram king of Judah went down to see Joram the son of Ahab in Jezreel, because he was sick.
